security_gap.gap_description: Proposal authenticity not verified, allowing submission by unauthorized parties.

actor_risk.description: Actor requires sufficient BGT/delegation to propose; risk exists if this check is bypassed.

behavioral_risk.rationale: If proposal submission lacks identity and BGT ownership/delegation verification, unauthorized proposals could be submitted.

assumptions: The governance contract does not strictly verify the identity and BGT holding/delegation of the proposer.
Proposal submission is possible without proper authentication.

mitigation.strategy: Implement strong identity verification and BGT/delegation checks for proposal submission.

description: An attacker may impersonate a legitimate BGT holder or delegator to submit unauthorized governance proposals.

security_gap.gap_description: Proposal data can be altered during transit or processing.

actor_risk.description: Attacker needs ability to intercept or modify data flow.

behavioral_risk.rationale: If proposal data is not integrity-protected end-to-end, it could be maliciously modified.

assumptions: The data flow for submitting proposals is not protected against modification (e.g., unsigned or not hashed end-to-end).

mitigation.strategy: Implement cryptographic integrity checks (e.g., signing, hashing) for proposal data.

description: An attacker may intercept and modify the content or parameters of a governance proposal during its submission.

security_gap.gap_description: Governance system can be overwhelmed by excessive or invalid proposals.

actor_risk.description: Actor can potentially submit proposals without significant cost or validation.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Lack of sufficient barriers or validation on proposal submission can lead to spam or resource exhaustion.

assumptions: Submitting a proposal does not require a high cost (e.g., gas fee relative to attack cost).
Validation checks for proposals are insufficient to filter out large volumes of malicious/spam submissions.

mitigation.strategy: Implement dynamic gas fees for proposal submission, rate limiting, and strict validation checks.

description: An attacker may flood the governance contract with numerous proposals, disrupting the normal operation and review process.

security_gap.gap_description: Vote authenticity not verified, allowing unauthorized votes to influence outcome.

actor_risk.description: Actor requires BGT holding/delegation to vote; risk exists if this check is bypassed.

behavioral_risk.rationale: If voting lacks verification of the voter's identity and BGT/delegation status, vote integrity is compromised.

assumptions: The governance contract does not strictly verify the identity and BGT holding/delegation of the voter.
Voting is possible without proper authentication or signature verification.

mitigation.strategy: Implement strong identity verification, BGT/delegation checks, and signature verification for voting.

description: An attacker may impersonate a legitimate BGT holder or delegator to cast fraudulent votes on governance proposals.

security_gap.gap_description: Vote data can be altered during transit or processing.

actor_risk.description: Attacker needs ability to intercept or modify data flow.

behavioral_risk.rationale: If vote data is not integrity-protected end-to-end, it could be maliciously modified.

assumptions: The data flow for submitting votes is not protected against modification (e.g., unsigned or not hashed end-to-end).

mitigation.strategy: Implement cryptographic integrity checks (e.g., signing) for vote data.

description: An attacker may intercept and modify a user's vote (e.g., change 'yes' to 'no') during its submission.

security_gap.gap_description: A user can cast a vote and later deny having done so.

actor_risk.description: Applies to any user casting a vote.

behavioral_risk.rationale: While possible, non-repudiation of votes is often less critical than integrity or availability in public governance.

assumptions: The link between the on-chain vote transaction and the user's off-chain identity or private key is not definitively proven and recorded.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure all vote transactions are cryptographically signed by the user's key controlling the voting power.

description: A user who casts a vote on a proposal might later be able to deny their participation.

security_gap.gap_description: Voting system can be disrupted by excessive or invalid vote submissions.

actor_risk.description: Actor can potentially submit votes without significant cost or validation.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Lack of sufficient barriers or validation on vote submission can lead to spam or resource exhaustion.

assumptions: Casting a vote does not require a high cost (e.g., gas fee relative to attack cost).
Validation checks for votes are insufficient to filter out large volumes of malicious/spam submissions.

mitigation.strategy: Implement dynamic gas fees for voting, rate limiting, and strict validation checks.

description: An attacker may flood the governance contract with numerous invalid votes, disrupting the voting process or tally.

security_gap.gap_description: Liquidity provision details can be altered during transit or processing.

actor_risk.description: Attacker needs ability to intercept or modify data flow.

behavioral_risk.rationale: If deposit data is not integrity-protected end-to-end, a user's intended liquidity provision could be altered.

assumptions: The data flow for providing liquidity is not protected against modification (e.g., unsigned or not hashed end-to-end).

mitigation.strategy: Implement cryptographic integrity checks (e.g., signing) for liquidity provision data.

description: An attacker may intercept and modify the amounts or token types in a user's liquidity provision transaction to BEX.

security_gap.gap_description: BEX liquidity provision can be disrupted by excessive or invalid transactions.

actor_risk.description: Actor can potentially submit transactions without significant cost or validation.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Lack of sufficient barriers or validation on liquidity provision can lead to spam or resource exhaustion.

assumptions: Providing liquidity does not require a high cost (e.g., gas fee relative to attack cost).
Validation checks for liquidity provision are insufficient to filter out large volumes of malicious/spam submissions.

mitigation.strategy: Implement dynamic gas fees, rate limiting, and strict validation checks for liquidity provision.

description: An attacker may flood BEX with numerous small or invalid liquidity provision transactions, disrupting pool operations.

security_gap.gap_description: LP staking details can be altered during transit or processing.

actor_risk.description: Attacker needs ability to intercept or modify data flow.

behavioral_risk.rationale: If staking data is not integrity-protected end-to-end, a user's intended stake could be altered.

assumptions: The data flow for staking LP tokens is not protected against modification (e.g., unsigned or not hashed end-to-end).

mitigation.strategy: Implement cryptographic integrity checks (e.g., signing) for staking data.

description: An attacker may intercept and modify the amount of LP tokens staked or the target Reward Vault during submission.

security_gap.gap_description: Reward Vault staking can be disrupted by excessive or invalid transactions.

actor_risk.description: Actor can potentially submit transactions without significant cost or validation.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Lack of sufficient barriers or validation on staking can lead to spam or resource exhaustion.

assumptions: Staking LP tokens does not require a high cost (e.g., gas fee relative to attack cost).
Validation checks for staking are insufficient to filter out large volumes of malicious/spam submissions.

mitigation.strategy: Implement dynamic gas fees, rate limiting, and strict validation checks for staking.

description: An attacker may flood Reward Vaults with numerous small or invalid staking transactions, disrupting reward calculations or claims.

security_gap.gap_description: Delegation authenticity not verified, allowing unauthorized delegation or undelegation.

actor_risk.description: Actor requires BGT holding/control to delegate; risk exists if this check is bypassed.

behavioral_risk.rationale: If delegation lacks verification of the delegator's identity and BGT ownership, delegation integrity is compromised.

assumptions: The delegation mechanism does not strictly verify the identity and BGT holding/control of the delegator.
Delegation is possible without proper authentication or signature verification.

mitigation.strategy: Implement strong identity verification, BGT ownership checks, and signature verification for delegation.

description: An attacker may impersonate a legitimate BGT holder to delegate or undelegate their BGT to or from a validator.

security_gap.gap_description: Delegation details can be altered during transit or processing.

actor_risk.description: Attacker needs ability to intercept or modify data flow.

behavioral_risk.rationale: If delegation data is not integrity-protected end-to-end, a user's intended delegation could be altered.

assumptions: The data flow for delegating BGT is not protected against modification (e.g., unsigned or not hashed end-to-end).

mitigation.strategy: Implement cryptographic integrity checks (e.g., signing) for delegation data.

description: An attacker may intercept and modify the amount of BGT delegated or the target validator during submission.

security_gap.gap_description: Delegation system can be disrupted by excessive or invalid transactions.

actor_risk.description: Actor can potentially submit transactions without significant cost or validation.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Lack of sufficient barriers or validation on delegation can lead to spam or resource exhaustion, affecting Boost calculations.

assumptions: Delegating BGT does not require a high cost (e.g., gas fee relative to attack cost).
Validation checks for delegation are insufficient to filter out large volumes of malicious/spam submissions.

mitigation.strategy: Implement dynamic gas fees, rate limiting, and strict validation checks for delegation.

description: An attacker may flood the delegation mechanism with numerous small or invalid transactions, disrupting boost calculations.

attack_surface.data_description: Claim request data (amount, token type, proof)

security_gap.gap_description: Claim authenticity not verified, allowing unauthorized claiming of others' rewards.

actor_risk.description: Actor requires a valid claim proof; risk exists if proofs can be forged or reused.

behavioral_risk.rationale: If the claim proof mechanism is vulnerable, attackers could steal user rewards.

assumptions: The claim proof generation or verification mechanism is vulnerable to forgery or replay attacks.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure claim proofs are cryptographically secure, single-use, and strictly verified on-chain against user identity.

description: An attacker may use a forged or replayed claim proof to impersonate a user and steal their earned BGT or Incentive tokens.

attack_surface.data_description: Claim request data (amount, token type, proof)

security_gap.gap_description: Claim details can be altered during transit or processing.

actor_risk.description: Attacker needs ability to intercept or modify data flow.

behavioral_risk.rationale: If claim data is not integrity-protected end-to-end and linked to the proof, a user's claim could be altered.

assumptions: The data flow for claiming rewards is not protected against modification, or the proof doesn't cover all claim parameters.

mitigation.strategy: Implement cryptographic integrity checks (e.g., signing) and ensure proof covers all critical claim parameters.

description: An attacker may intercept and modify the amount or type of tokens being claimed by a user.

attack_surface.data_description: Claim request data (amount, token type, proof)

security_gap.gap_description: Claiming mechanism can be disrupted by excessive or invalid transactions.

actor_risk.description: Actor can potentially submit transactions without significant cost or validation.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Lack of sufficient barriers or validation on claiming can lead to spam or resource exhaustion.

assumptions: Claiming rewards does not require a high cost (e.g., gas fee relative to attack cost).
Validation checks for claims are insufficient to filter out large volumes of malicious/spam submissions.

mitigation.strategy: Implement dynamic gas fees, rate limiting, and strict validation checks for claiming.

description: An attacker may flood the claiming mechanism with numerous invalid claims, preventing legitimate users from accessing their rewards.

attack_surface.data_description: Swap request data (amounts, token types, slippage)

security_gap.gap_description: Swap parameters can be altered during transit or processing, leading to unfavorable trades.

actor_risk.description: Attacker needs ability to intercept or modify data flow.

behavioral_risk.rationale: If swap data is not integrity-protected, an attacker could manipulate user trades, causing financial loss (e.g., via sandwich attacks or simple modification).

assumptions: The data flow for submitting swap requests is not protected against modification (e.g., unsigned or not hashed end-to-end).
The swap contract doesn't strictly enforce the integrity of the request parameters received.

mitigation.strategy: Implement cryptographic integrity checks (e.g., signing) for swap request data.

description: An attacker may intercept and modify a user's swap request (amounts, tokens, slippage) before it is executed on BEX.

attack_surface.data_description: Swap request data (amounts, token types, slippage)

security_gap.gap_description: BEX trading can be disrupted by excessive or invalid transactions.

actor_risk.description: Actor can potentially submit transactions without significant cost or validation.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Lack of sufficient barriers or validation on swaps can lead to spam or resource exhaustion, affecting trading availability and user experience.

assumptions: Executing a swap does not require a high cost (e.g., gas fee relative to attack cost).
Validation checks for swaps are insufficient to filter out large volumes of malicious/spam submissions.

mitigation.strategy: Implement dynamic gas fees, rate limiting, and strict validation checks for swaps.

description: An attacker may flood BEX with numerous small or invalid swap transactions, disrupting trading and increasing network congestion.
